verification.note: Reported by IQFM (Mashaal, Abou-Zeid, arXiv:2506.06718), LoRA few-shot (up to 200 samples/class): 96.05% on RF fingerprinting (vs 96.64% supervised), the abstract's headline figure, verbatim (Fig 9a curve; verified via pdftotext). k_shot=200 is the paper's max samples/class for POWDER. POWDER RF fingerprinting = 4 base stations (USRP X310, IEEE 802.11a) on the POWDER PAWR platform (Reus-Muns et al. GLOBECOM 2020), the SAME dataset as our board's POWDER site-ID column. NOT reproduced by this repo. from_paper_UNCERTAIN: (1) FEW-SHOT regime (frozen/adapted FM + light head), a different regime column from our from_scratch baselines; (2) the FM uses the 802.11a WiFi subset whereas our closed_set pools 4G/5G/WiFi waveforms (same-day, single day); (3) split indices unpublished. Same dataset + task (BS identification) + metric (rank1 accuracy), regime/subset not byte-confirmed identical -> uncertain. References our committed POWDER same-day split; a literature coverage point for the FM, never conflated with an in-repo run. Model size: n_params=341800 (high confidence — arXiv:2506.06718 (IQFM), Section V-A: "Our implementation, based on the official PyTorch release, contains approximately 341k trainable parameters" and "the ful…)
